heading: Restore Considerations
body:
In addition to the [prerequisites](restore-prereq), consider the
following requirements and limitations when restoring from a
locally-downloaded snapshot.

- If the `DefaultRWConcern` value on the source snapshot differs
  from the `DefaultRWConcern` value on the target
  cluster, Atlas overrides the value on the source
  snapshot with the value on the target cluster. If
  there is no value configured for the `DefaultRWConcern` on the
  target cluster, Atlas keeps the value of
  `DefaultRWConcern` from the snapshot without explicit
  configuration. This may differ from the default value for that
  MongoDB version.

* This feature is unavailable for Free clusters (formerly known as `M0`).

* The downloaded files consist of the raw files copied from the
  `data` directory. `mongorestore` is incompatible with these
  files. To access the data files, use [the following procedure](restore-local-snapshot-procedure) to start a [mongod](manual/reference/program/mongod)
  instance and point it to the extract directory.

* If you are at or near your cloud provider's [Learn About Private Endpoints in Atlas](private-endpoint-overview), you might not be able to download a
  snapshot through a private endpoint. In this case, use the public
  download endpoint that Atlas provides.
